likes: Being able to get a home visit when the dog was too worked up to travel
The vet took time to actually explain things instead of rushing me out
dislikes: Never sure who's answering the phone or if they remember us from last time
Had to repeat the dog's whole history over the phone before they'd even book me in
Waiting on hold when I'm already anxious about why I'm calling
jobs_to_be_done: Ringing the clinic directly for anything at all, including questions I could probably Google
Asking the Facebook mammy group locally what's normal before deciding if it's worth a vet visit
Writing reminders on the kitchen calendar for jabs and check-ups
Googling symptoms late at night when I'm worried, then usually ringing the clinic the next morning anyway
rejections: Pollen & air-quality alerts for allergic pets
quotes: I'm not great with apps, I'd honestly rather just ring and hear a real person tell me he's grand.
Last time I had to explain his whole history again on the phone, that's the bit that gets to me, not knowing if they remember us.
A pet horoscope, are you serious, I've enough going on without that.

likes: The vets themselves seem to know what they're doing once you're actually in the room
They did a home visit once when my dog was too sore to move, that was decent of them
dislikes: Getting through on the phone takes forever, especially first thing Monday
The out-of-hours emergency consult hit me with a price I wasn't warned about beforehand
Nobody tells you the price until you're already sitting there with the dog on the table
Feels like every visit somehow turns into 'and we'd recommend this extra thing too', which adds up fast
jobs_to_be_done: Ringing around other Dublin vets to compare prices before booking anything non-urgent
Googling symptoms myself at 11pm before deciding if it's a real emergency or not
Asking in the local Donnybrook/Ranelagh Facebook group who's cheap and who's not
Keeping a note on my phone of when jabs and worming were last done because nobody reminds me
rejections: pollen & air-quality alerts for allergic pets
quotes: I'd say I ring the clinic more than I chat with anything online, I just don't trust an app to actually answer when the dog's in bother.
If it could tell me roughly what a visit's going to cost before I walk in the door, I'd actually open it. That out-of-hours bill still stings.
The horoscope and the NFT thing, would you ever, that's not what I'm paying a vet for.

likes: The vet who did the puppy course was thorough and didn't rush me
They got the microchip and kennel cough vaccine sorted in one visit so I didn't have to come back twice
dislikes: Waiting on hold to book a slot, some days it just rings out
Not told the total cost upfront, the bill for the puppy course crept up each visit
Reception asking me to repeat details I'd already given them the visit before
jobs_to_be_done: Googling whether something is urgent before ringing the clinic
WhatsApp group with other dog owners nearby for 'is this normal' questions
Checking Met Eireann myself before walks, nobody at the clinic told me
Keeping a note on my phone of when the next jabs are due because nobody reminded me

feature_requests.verbatim: If it just told me straight what a visit's going to cost before I go in, that'd save the phone calls back and forth
Something to remind me when his next jab or worming is due, I keep forgetting

quotes: I'd still ring for an actual appointment, I'm not booking my dog's health online, but if it pinged me when his jabs are due, that I'd actually use.
The prices crept up every time I went in and nobody told me what it'd cost before we started, that's what annoyed me, not the chatbot.
A horoscope for the dog, come on now, I'm trying to save money, not spend it on nonsense.

dislikes: Being kept waiting for callbacks when it's about her dog's heart, especially after an ultrasound
Having to re-explain the dog's full history every time a different vet picks up the phone
Getting a bill that wasn't flagged in advance after an unplanned emergency stabilisation

feature_requests.verbatim: if it could just tell me straight away whether what I'm looking at is 'ring the emergency line now' or 'fine till Monday', that alone would be worth opening the app for
some kind of running summary of Bisto's file, so I'm not repeating the same three paragraphs to whoever's on shift

jobs_to_be_done: Googling symptoms at 11pm before deciding whether it's an emergency
Keeping my own notes app with dates of scans, meds, and vet advice because the clinic doesn't hand me a running record

likes: The vets themselves are good, thorough, they don't rush you
Being able to get a second opinion when something didn't sit right with me
They've done right by Bisto through some scary moments, the emergency stabilisation in particular
quotes: With a Cavalier this age, it's not routine checkups, it's heart stuff, it's real. I don't need a gimmick, I need to know whether tonight's the night I panic or not.
Twelve visits in two years, I'm not exaggerating when I say I know that waiting room better than my own kitchen.
Pet horoscope? For God's sake, no. I'm dealing with mitral valve disease, not star signs.
rejections: lost-pet microchip checklist (Bisto's never left my sight, not what I need)

likes: The vets themselves are good with Purrl (the cat) even though she hates being handled, especially post-dental

They fit us in as a walk-in when we've turned up worried, they don't turn you away
dislikes: The telehealth video consult was a bit of a waste of time, the vet basically said 'bring her in' anyway so we paid for two visits to get to the same place

Nobody proactively told us she's due more frequent senior checks now she's over 12, we've had to ask ourselves

quotes: The telehealth thing just meant we paid twice to be told 'bring her in', so I'm not exactly sold on more tech.
